name: Bin Packing & Resource Waste
goal: Prove solver reduces BM touched count and preserves future-fit capacity, vs round-robin scattering VMs across all BMs.
hypothesis: RR rotates through BMs 1-VM-at-a-time and does not sort VMs by size. With 15 VMs and 10 BMs, RR touches ALL 10 BMs (each gets 1~2 VMs) and leaves zero clean machines for a follow-up cluster. Solver packs the same workload into ~3-4 BMs, preserving 6+ fully empty BMs for future placements. This is invariant to RR's BM iteration order.

inventory_setup.description: 10 identical empty BMs spread across 3 AGs. No placeholders — clean slate lets the bin-packing difference show without confounding noise.

anti_affinity_rules.note: Both RR and Solver should satisfy this. Included for realism; not the differentiator in S2.

capacity_math.minimum_bms_note: ceil(216/64) = 4. Solver should hit this or one above.
capacity_math.slack_note: 6 BMs are 'future capacity' — whichever scheduler leaves more of them clean, wins.

execution_steps: 1. Create 3 ags / 10 racks / 10 BMs in inventory (empty).
2. Confirm no VMs exist on any bm-s2-* before starting.
3. Call POST /api/v1/schedule (RR) with the test_request. Record full mapping.
4. Delete the 15 test VMs created by v1 (scenario reset — BMs should be empty again).
5. Call POST /api/v2/scheduler (solver) with the same test_request. Record full mapping.

v1_round_robin.bm_touched_note: Rotate behavior + 15 VMs > 10 BMs guarantees every BM receives at least 1 VM.
v1_round_robin.largest_remaining_free_slot_cpu: At most 48c (a BM with just one 16c worker). No BM remains at 64c.
v1_round_robin.verdict: PASS placement, FAIL future-fit — any follow-up cluster needing a full 64c BM is blocked.

v2_solver.bm_touched_count: 3 or 4 (minimum 4 by arithmetic; solver may choose 4 for slack).
v2_solver.verdict: PASS placement, PASS future-fit — 6+ clean BMs remain available.

expected_outcome.delta_story: Same workload, same AG correctness. RR consumed 10 BMs of scheduling 'real estate'; solver consumed 4. If a second cluster arrives needing even a single 64c VM, RR pool cannot serve it without displacing existing workload. Solver pool can serve 6 more.

metrics_derivable_from_mapping: per_bm_used_cpu = sum(vm.demand.cpu for vm mapped to that bm)
largest_remaining_free_slot_cpu = max(64 - per_bm_used_cpu) over all 10 BMs
headroom_variance = variance of (64 - per_bm_used_cpu) across all 10 BMs
master_ag_distribution = count of masters per AG (via bm -> rack -> ag lookup)
